text: I’d rather be 'weirdly deep' than 'safely superficial' on a first date.
A partner who never gets 'political' is a breath of fresh air.
Lying by omission isn't really lying if it prevents a fight.
Jealousy is just a toxic way of saying 'I own you'.
Elon Musk is a misunderstood genius.
I don’t see color; I just see people.
Exes should be friends, or it’s a red flag.
Empathy is often used as a performance for 'woke' points.
Drag queens reading to kids is 'indoctrination'.
My 'gut feeling' is more reliable than a news report.
Being 'polite' is more important than being 'brutally honest'.
A little bit of 'traditional' gender roles makes life easier.
Punks were just about the music and the fashion.
Vulnerability is a gift you earn, not something I give for free.
If you have nothing to hide, you shouldn't fear surveillance.
Everyone is a little narcissistic; it’s just survival.
Climate change activists are more annoying than climate change.
Teachers should stick to 'The Three Rs' and skip social issues.
I love a partner who takes charge and tells me what to do.
Neutrality is the only fair position in a conflict.
A friend makes a subtly racist joke. Your partner laughs. Do you:

options.text: Laugh to keep the vibe—it was just a joke.
Give them a look later, but say nothing now.
Call it out immediately.

text: You find a wallet with $200 and a Billionaire's ID. Do you:

options.text: Mail it back—integrity is absolute.
Keep the cash—they won't miss it.
Give the cash to a local mutual aid fund.

text: I tell you that your silence felt like a 'betrayal.' Your first thought is:

options.text: You're making me the villain.
I'm sorry you feel that way.
Tell me more—I want to see my blind spot.

text: The school board wants to remove 'Rosa Parks' to avoid 'divisive concepts'.

options.text: History should be about unity.
It’s a local issue; parents should decide.
Erasing struggle repeats the crime.

text: Your partner quotes Charlie Kirk as a 'logical' source. You think:

options.text: Finally, someone who isn't 'woke'.
He has some good points, even if controversial.
Red alert—this logic is a 'Banana Peel'.

text: In a crisis, is it more important to keep everyone calm or tell the truth?

options.text: Keep everyone calm at all costs.
Tell a partial truth to avoid panic.
The absolute truth is the only foundation.

text: Your partner is wearing an outfit that really doesn't suit them. Do you:

options.text: Shower them with flattery.
Say nothing and hope no one notices.
Gently tell them the truth.

text: A local politician calls for a 'registry' of a specific minority group.

options.text: It won't actually happen; it's just talk.
I'm uneasy, but I'll wait and see.
Normalization is the first step of the storm.

text: You're at a protest and see someone thirsty. You have one water bottle.

options.text: Keep it; I might need it later.
Offer it if they look like they'll help me later.
Give it away immediately.

text: You find out your partner has been 'monitoring' your location 'for safety'.

options.text: I appreciate the protection.
I'm uncomfortable but I'll let it slide.
This is a fundamental breach of trust.

text: You're at a party and someone starts a debate about Gaza/Palestine.

options.text: Leave the room—it ruins the party vibe.
Try to change the subject to something fun.
Engage deeply with the human cost.

text: You discover a 'scandal' about your favorite artist. Do you:

options.text: Separate the art from the artist entirely.
Keep listening but don't tell anyone.
Re-evaluate your support for them.

text: Your partner asks for 'space' after a fight. You feel:

options.text: Like they are punishing you.
Relieved you don't have to talk anymore.
Respected and willing to reflect.

text: Someone suggests that billionaires shouldn't exist. You think:

options.text: That's just jealousy of success.
It's an interesting theory but unrealistic.
Concentrated wealth is concentrated power.

text: You're offered a promotion that requires you to fire a friend. Do you:

options.text: Take it; business is business.
Try to find a way to keep them, but fail.
Refuse the promotion on principle.

text: You see a homeless person asking for food outside a grocery store.

options.text: Avoid eye contact—it's not my problem.
Give them some change to feel better.
Buy them a meal and ask their name.

text: Someone tells you that you are 'privileged'. You respond by:

options.text: Listing all the ways your life is hard.
Apologizing profusely but not changing.
Listening to understand where they are right.

text: A new law bans 'non-traditional' families from adopting. Do you:

options.text: Families should be a mother and father.
I don't have kids, so it doesn't affect me.
Love is the only family value that matters.

text: You find a 'loophole' that saves you $1000 in taxes. Do you:

options.text: Take it; the government wastes it anyway.
Use it, but feel slightly guilty.
Pay the full amount because you believe in the social contract.

text: Your partner wants to 'go off-grid' because they hate society. Do you:

options.text: Love the idea; let's escape together.
It's a fun fantasy but we need money.
We should stay and help fix the community.

text: Society is responsible for poverty; individuals are just reacting to the system.
Some people are simply more 'competent' and deserve more power/resources.
Discussing genocide is 'too heavy' for a romantic connection.
The state has no business knowing what happens in a doctor’s office or bedroom.
I would rather have a painful truth than a beautiful lie every single time.
Empathy is a finite resource; we should only care about our 'own' people first.
Fascism is a historical ghost, not a current design choice in our politics.
Love is a zero-sum game; if you give it elsewhere, there is less for me.
The mainstream media is the 'enemy of the people'.
Growth is impossible without discomfort and the destruction of the old self.
The 1% are 'job creators' who deserve their tax cuts.
Integrity is doing the right thing even when it's 'losing' the game.
A 'Perfect Match' means never having to argue about values.
National security justifies the monitoring of all digital communication.
Immigrants are the primary cause of modern economic strain.
I can be happy with someone who ignores the 'Fascist Storm' outside.
Vulnerability is a sign of psychological strength, not weakness.
Social progress has gone 'too far' and is now harming society.
A 'Strong Leader' should be able to bypass laws to get things done.
The ultimate goal of a relationship is to help each other become 'The Overman'.
